title: Generate All Possible Pairs of Balanced Parenthese

content.problemStatement:
Given a number nnn, generate all possible nnn pairs of balanced parentheses.  A balanced parentheses string has opening and closing parentheses in a way that every opening parenthesis has a matching closing parenthesis and the pairs are properly nested.

You simply need to complete the provided function and don’t worry about the main function.


Input Format
The input contains a single integer nnn.



Output Format
Generate all combinations of nnn pairs of balanced parentheses. Printing of the brackets will be done by the main function.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 13) representing the number of pairs of parentheses to generate.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output all distinct balanced parentheses strings of length 2·n on one line, separated by a single space, in the order produced by a standard back‑tracking algorithm that always tries to add '(' before ')'. Print a newline after the output of each test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Three test cases. For n=3 the five balanced strings are printed in back‑tracking order, for n=2 the two strings, and for n=1 the single pair.
n=4 yields the 14 Catalan strings; they are listed in the order the algorithm generates them.
First test case prints the only string for n=1, second test case prints the five strings for n=3.

solution.approach: We generate the strings using depth‑first backtracking. While building a partial string we keep two counters: the number of '(' placed (open) and the number of ')' placed (close). At any step we may add '(' if open < n, and we may add ')' if close < open (to keep the string valid). When open == n and close == n the current string is complete and is stored. Because we always try '(' before ')', the produced order matches the required back‑tracking order. The algorithm runs in O(C_n) time where C_n is the n‑th Catalan number (the number of valid strings) and uses O(n) auxiliary stack space for recursion.
solution.timeComplexity: O(C_n) where C_n is the n‑th Catalan number (≈ 4^n / (n^{3/2} √π)).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(C_n * n) for storing all results plus O(n) recursion depth.
